source: National Minimum Wage Act 9 of 2018 — 2026 determination by Minister of Employment and Labour
data_status: CONFIRMED — sourced from Department of Employment and Labour official NMW flyer, Revised February 2026. Effective 1 March 2026.

general_workers.note: CONFIRMED. Applies to all workers including farm workers and domestic workers. Source: Dept of Employment and Labour NMW flyer revised February 2026.

domestic_workers.note: CONFIRMED. Equalised to general NMW rate. Same R30.23/hour as all other workers.

farm_workers.note: CONFIRMED. Equalised to general NMW rate. Same R30.23/hour as all other workers.

expanded_public_works_programme.note: CONFIRMED. EPWP exempted sector rate R16.62/hour. Source: Dept of Employment and Labour NMW flyer revised February 2026.

eti_relevance.description: The ETI qualification check requires the employee to be paid at least the NMW applicable to their sector. Use general_workers.hourly_rate for standard employees at Zenowethu.
eti_relevance.note: CONFIRMED. R30.23 × 160 hours = R4,836.80/month. An employee earning below this amount does not qualify for ETI even if they meet age criteria (18-29). The ETI tier floor of R2,500 is a separate Act schedule amount, not the NMW.

hours_reference.note: 195 hours/month = 45h/week × 52/12. 160 hours/month is the ETI standard used for pro-ration.

enforcement.employer_obligation: Employer may not pay below NMW. NMW applies regardless of how remuneration is structured (hourly, weekly, monthly).
enforcement.sectoral_determinations: Where a sectoral minimum wage under the Employment Conditions Commission is higher than NMW, the sectoral rate prevails.
enforcement.bcea_link: Failure to pay NMW constitutes an unfair labour practice and a BCEA violation.
